mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that sustains corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signal supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when applied outside post-shock recovery periods or during steady-state expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions suppress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in over 60% of periods where the federal funds rate remains above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for payroll growth falls below 0.02 or forward returns show no significant positive correlation with employment gains.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates weak consumer demand relative to output, creating inventory overhang risks that trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of cases or Rank IC remains below 0.015 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase confirms supply chain normalization and capacity utilization improvements, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative forward returns in over 55% of post-shock recovery periods.
